task_desc: Fix Brain3D eager WebGL initialization at page load wasting GPU resources and always-active resize listener -- Two resource management issues in the Brain3D module: (1) main.js:426-428 calls Brain3D.init() unconditionally at page load, creating a full WebGL context, renderer, PerspectiveCamera, scene with 10 region meshes (23 total geometries) plus wireframe sphere, OrbitControls, raycaster, and registering window resize + canvas mousemove event listeners -- all before the user ever clicks "Brain 3D"; the container div has display:none at init time so clientWidth/clientHeight are 0 and the fallback window dimensions are used for the renderer; Brain3D.show() at brain3d.js:263-272 already has lazy init logic (if !_initialized then init()) that would correctly initialize on first toggle, so removing the eager init at main.js:426-428 makes initialization lazy and saves GPU memory and a WebGL context on mobile devices where context limits can be as low as 8-16 total, (2) brain3d.js:174 registers window.addEventListener('resize', Brain3D._onResize) in init() but never removes it; _onResize recalculates camera.aspect, calls camera.updateProjectionMatrix(), and calls renderer.setSize() on every window resize even when the overlay is hidden (display:none); on mobile devices orientation changes, keyboard show/hide, and browser chrome resize all trigger this unnecessary work; fix by adding the resize listener in show() and removing it in hide() so it only fires while the overlay is visible. Files: js/main.js, js/brain3d.js.
